author: The Incarnation of the Word
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.

Here again, was God to keep silence? to allow to false gods the worship He made us to render to Himself? A king whose subjects had revolted would, after sending letters and messages, go to them in person. How much more shall God restore in us the grace of His image. This men, themselves but copies, could not do. Hence the Word Himself must come (1) to recreate, (2) to destroy death in the Body.

ccel.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

title: Here again, was God to keep silence? to allow to false gods the worship He made us to render to Himself? A king whose subjects had revolted would, after sending letters and messages, go to them in person. How much more shall God restore in us the grace of His image. This men, themselves but copies, could not do. Hence the Word Himself must come (1) to recreate, (2) to destroy death in the Body.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Here again, was God to keep silence? to allow to false gods the worship He made us to render to Himself?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: A king whose subjects had revolted would, after sending letters and messages, go to them in person.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: How much more shall God restore in us the grace of His image.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: This men, themselves but copies, could not do.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Hence the Word Himself must come (1) to recreate, (2) to destroy death in the Body.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: So then, men having thus become brutalized, and demoniacal deceit thus clouding every place, and hiding the knowledge of the true God, what was God to do?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: To keep still silence at so great a thing, and suffer men to be led astray by demons and not to know God?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And what was the use of man having been originally made in God’s image?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For it had been better for him to have been made simply like a brute animal, than, once made rational, for him to live the life of the brutes.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or where was any necessity at all for his receiving the idea of God to begin with?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For if he be not fit to receive it even now, it were better it had not been given him at first.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or what profit to God Who has made them, or what glory to Him could it be, if men, made by Him, do not worship Him, but think that others are their makers?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For God thus proves to have made these for others instead of for Himself.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Once again, a merely human king does not let the lands he has colonized pass to others to serve them, nor go over to other men; but he warns them by letters, and often sends to them by friends, or, if need be, he comes in person, to put them to rebuke in the last resort by his presence, only that they may not serve others and his own work be spent for naught.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Shall not God much more spare His own creatures, that they be not led astray from Him and serve things of nought? especially since such going astray proves the cause of their ruin and undoing, and since it was unfitting that they should perish which had once been partakers of God’s image.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: What then was God to do? or what was to be done save the renewing of that which was in God’s image, so that by it men might once more be able to know Him?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But how could this have come to pass save by the presence of the very Image of God, our Lord Jesus Christ?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For by men’s means it was impossible, since they are but made after an image; nor by angels either, for not even they are (God’s) images.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Whence the Word of God came in His own person, that, as He was the Image of the Father, He might be able to create afresh the man after the image.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But, again, it could not else have taken place had not death and corruption been done away.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Whence He took, in natural fitness, a mortal body, that while death might in it be once for all done away, men made after His Image might once more be renewed.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: None other then was sufficient for this need, save the Image of the Father.
